entry.title: Compass: Predictive Range Proposal "Smart Boundaries"
entry.content:
Introduction

For SAP Analytics Cloud, version 2026.12, we have integrated the capability of Smart Predict into compass to generate forecast of possible driver fluctuations with one single click. Customers on quarterly release will enjoy this benefit from QRC3 onwards. With the integration, users can automatically generate boundaries for driver fluctuations based on analysis of the historical data, without the hassle of technical setup.

One-Click-Trigger to insert predicted ranges for driver fluctuations

 

 

 

How Does it Work?

Smart Boundaries can be triggered by a new button on the drivers list, with two options for predicted results: Either as absolute numbers or as relative percentages. Once triggered, a time series analysis of the available historical data will begin and loading indicators will appear within the input fields for the min/max values, concluding by feeding the results directly into the input fields once ready. You can then proceed to run the scenario with the suggested boundaries, or fine-tune the results manually first, if preferred. A prediction process can be aborted any time before its conclusion by clicking on the same button again.

Choose between absolute or relative values (%) for min/max boundaries

Range of Historical Data Used for Prediction
All historical data from the version used in the scenario will be analysed, up to the cut-off date, which is the configured data filter for the simulation driver. E.g. if the driver filter is “Gross Revenue Q4 2026” and the scenario is based on the version “Actuals”, then all available data for gross revenue in that version up to the last period before Q4 2026 will be taken into consideration. 
Note that the minimum ratio of historical data to result would be around 1:1, but depending on factors such as seasonality or data quality, more data might be required to produce results. Therefore, the recommended amount of historical data would be two to three years to capture cycles and trend.

 

Use Cases & Benefits

Generally, this feature is beneficial in cases where assistance is required for modelling uncertainty, including the following:

Understanding the Corresponding Risk of Underlying Trend: If keen to explore the question of "what are the risks if things continue the way as before", a scenario can be run using Smart Boundaries suggestions for key driver performances without adjustments, to simulate the compound effects for uncertainties across key drivers resulting from the historical development.
For this approach, only Smart Boundaries results are used to model key driver uncertainties.

Pre-configure Risk Guide Rails: Smart Boundaries can also be used to pre-populate the value configuration with guide rails derived from historical data, which can be used as a starting point to model mitigation strategy. This could be also particularly useful in cases where IT is creating a compass simulation to be handed over to business end users for further simulation. Instead of creating a simulation artifact with empty configurations for driver min/max values, IT could already pre-fill the driver list with Smart Boundaries results.
For this approach, end users will utilize Smart Boundaries insights and build on top of those by modifying them.

Accelerated Decision Intelligence: Often, a single user will not have the complete knowledge of possible fluctuation for all key drivers spanning across different regions, departments or line of businesses. Most likely, the end users are either experts in their domains or involved in discussions pertaining to drivers in their responsibilities and could model the uncertainties for those, but not the rest, for which they would need to seek insights from other topic experts or regions/departments. For situations where quick simulations are crucial to judge the current risk exposure and mitigation options, this could mean losing precious time for time-consuming alignments. In such cases Smart Boundaries could assist to fill in the blanks by modelling the uncertainties for drivers where the current trend is to be assumed.
For this approach, end users will be simulating with a mixture of drivers configured by Smart Boundaries and manual configurations.

 

FAQ

Is there an active link between the suggested boundaries and the model data?
No. Once generated, the boundaries are just any other input entry. It neither changes nor updates itself, and can be used as guide rails to be overwritten with new insights, if desired. 

Can I choose which range of data to use for the prediction?
No. Smart Boundaries will take all available data within the version used in the scenario, right up to the last period before the time filter definition for the driver. For example, if the driver is filtered down to Q1 2027, then all prior data in that version up to Q4 2026 will be used for the boundary prediction.

How much historical data would I need?
The minimum ratio is 1:1, so if generating predictions for a driver filtered down to Q1 2027, you will need at least one quarter of historical data. And if it is filtered down to 2027, you will need a year of data. But depending on the variability of your data, sometimes more data might be required to capture the seasonality and trend before results can be produced. Therefore a good rule of thumb is to have at least around two to three years of historical data.  

 

Conclusion

Smart Boundaries brings the best of both worlds together by using time series forecast to model driver uncertainties in compass. Depending on use case and usage, it can reveal corresponding risk for underlying the development trend, serve as guide rail for mitigation simulation, or provide fluctuations inputs for drivers where another source of insight is not at hand.

entry.title: Introducing Support Content — the additional Customer Support knowledge repository
entry.content:
Support Content is a curated knowledge hub that explains SAP systems from the perspective of Customer Support experts. Built from real Customer Support experience, it documents product logic, troubleshooting approaches, and recommended practices — helping you resolve issues faster and understand system behavior.

What you'll find there

Support Content provides practical information about SAP products and scenarios, including:

  • Explanations of system logic and expected behavior
  • Business process guidance and context
  • Step‑by‑step troubleshooting guides
  • Common configuration scenarios
  • Frequently asked questions (FAQs)
  • White papers and architecture examples
  • Best practices and recommendations

Content covers a broad range of SAP offerings — from newer topics such as SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP HANA Smart Data Integration to long‑standing products such as SAP Crystal Reports, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) — and everything that falls under Customer Support’s scope.

How it’s used and maintained

Support Content is designed to be a practical reference for customers. Entries are linked to other SAP repositories and are frequently referenced to speed diagnosis and resolution. The repository is reviewed and updated over time, with new topics added according to demand and evolving product landscapes.

How can you share feedback?

Feedback is welcome and helps the repository stay accurate and useful. Use the platform’s comments feature on each page to:

  • Report unclear or out‑of‑date information
  • Suggest additional scenarios or examples
  • Reference related support cases or product versions for context

You have two options to help us to improve the content:

  • Feedback (anonymous): using the Feedback feature on the right-hand side of the page, where you can tell us how helpful the content is and add an optional text.
  • Comment (need an authenticated user): you can provide your comments and be specific on which piece of the text you are referring to.
    First, select the words and click on the button:

    Then, you can add your comment:

* The Comment option will be gradually introduced on the topics. 

When commenting, include a brief description of the issue or the suggested change to help reviewers act more quickly.

Why use Support Content

  • Saves time by providing authoritative explanations and troubleshooting steps
  • Helps you understand root causes and expected system behavior
  • Complements other SAP knowledge sources by focusing on practical, support‑driven guidance

 Explore the Support Content to find product guidance, scenario examples, troubleshooting resources, and much more, tailored to help with your support needs and make the most of your SAP landscape.

Want to know where Support Content originates? Look at this blog post.

Cheers!!
Marcelo Pinheiro
Support Content Business Owner

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Planning: Where AI meets strategy and execution
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire introduced a new vision for Enterprise Planning, and it marks an important step forward for organizations looking to connect strategy with execution through AI.

If you work with SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning or SAP Business Data Cloud, this webinar will help you understand where these solutions are heading and why that matters for your customers. You'll see how AI agents, trusted business data, and managed business content come together to simplify planning, accelerate decision making, and create a more connected planning experience.

The thing is, planning has become much more than creating budgets or forecasts. It now means responding faster to change while keeping finance, operations, and strategy aligned.

Join us to explore SAP's roadmap for Enterprise Planning, discover the innovations announced at SAP Sapphire, and learn how today's SAP SAC and SAP Business Data Cloud investments are shaping the next generation of intelligent planning.

Register now and be among the first to explore what's next. https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/jun/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00014536.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #BusinessAI #SAPAnalyticsCloud #BusinessDataCloud #EnterprisePlanning

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Planning Now Available in Support Content Repository
entry.content:
What's New?

We're excited to announce a new dedicated topic SAP Analytics Cloud Planning in our Support Content repository! This addition brings together essential guidance on planning capabilities to help you make the most of your strategic initiatives.

What You'll Find There

When end users encounter issues with SAP Analytics Cloud Planning, for example a cell that won't accept input, a data action that writes nothing, they need guidance and answers that are not covered or beyond the standard Online Help and existing KBAs. That is exactly why we published this Support Content: written to bridge the gap between "something is wrong" and "here is exactly why, and here is what to do about it."

It captures the detailed configuration steps with screenshots, nuanced product behaviors, known limitations, and non-obvious root causes with samples, so that audience can understand, resolve issues faster and gain knowledge from the documentation.

The current SAC Planning Support Content covers the following topics:

  • Data Entry & Publishing - How the two-phase workflow protects data integrity, and what to check when users see inconsistent data between sessions.
  • Planning Area - Why configuring this feature in Model Settings can transform performance on large models, and the edge cases that affect cell inputability.
  • Data Locking & Data Access Control - How two independent gatekeepers interact, and the most common reasons edits are silently blocked.
  • Validation Rules & Member Derivation - Enforcing valid dimension combinations at the model level, including disaggregation behavior and current limits.
  • Data Actions - Queuing mechanics, the CONFIG.HIERARCHY setting that fixes the most common "zero records affected" mystery, and when to use synchronous mode.
  • Audit Log Management - Row limits, cleanup cadence recommendations, and version size impact.
  • Scripting Best Practices - API pitfalls around getMembers(), filter format mismatches, and the scripting patterns that cause render loops or unnecessary backend calls.

Whether you are an implementation consultant, customer administrator, or support engineer, this Support Content provides clear, actionable guidance to turn confusing problems into resolved ones, and it continues to evolve with new and refined topics.

Share Your Feedback

This is a growing resource! So if you have any questions about the content, please go ahead with the embedded feedback or comment feature.

Learn More About Support Content

The Support Content repository is the place to find content created and maintained by SAP Support engineers experties on their areas. Explore this repository with additional documentation provided to help the SAP ecosystem.

entry.title: Context Engineering in SAP Analytics Cloud: Building ContextWallet for AI and Genie Integrations
entry.content:
Introduction

 

 

 

Over the last few years, the conversation around analytics has shifted dramatically. Traditional dashboards are no longer viewed as the final destination for business intelligence. Instead, organizations are increasingly exploring how Artificial Intelligence can work alongside analytics platforms to provide conversational insights, automated explanations, and faster decision-making.

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) is already a powerful platform for planning, reporting, and analytics. At the same time, AI-powered solutions such as Databricks Genie and Large Language Models (LLMs) are enabling users to interact with data using natural language rather than traditional reports and dashboards.

However, while experimenting with SAC and AI integrations, I encountered a challenge that is rarely discussed but becomes immediately apparent once you start building enterprise AI solutions.

The challenge is not generating answers.

The challenge is providing the AI with the right business context.

Imagine a finance analyst working inside a SAC dashboard. Before analyzing any KPI, the analyst typically applies several business filters such as:

  • Company Code

  • Fiscal Year

  • Version

  • Currency

  • Profit Center

  • Account Type

These selections define the meaning of every chart, table, and KPI displayed on the dashboard. In many cases, the same metric can produce completely different interpretations depending on the selected filters.

Now consider a scenario where the analyst asks an AI assistant:

Why is EBITDA decreasing?

For a human looking at the dashboard, the question is obvious because the context is already visible on the screen. The user knows which company, fiscal year, and reporting version are currently selected.

For the AI, however, the question is incomplete.

Without access to the dashboard context, the AI does not know:

  • Which company is being analyzed

  • Which fiscal year is selected

  • Which reporting version is active

  • Which currency is being used

  • Which business entity is currently in focus

As a result, organizations often face a disconnect between analytics systems and AI systems. The dashboard contains the business context, while the AI receives only the question.

This observation led me to think about a concept that I call Context Engineering.

Just as Prompt Engineering focuses on designing effective instructions for AI systems, Context Engineering focuses on ensuring that AI systems receive the right business context before generating a response.

In enterprise analytics, context is often more important than the question itself. A perfectly crafted prompt can still produce an inaccurate answer if the AI lacks awareness of the user's current analytical context.

To explore this idea further, I started building a custom SAP Analytics Cloud widget called ContextWallet.

The goal of ContextWallet is simple:

Capture, preserve, and share dashboard context across SAC applications, pages, and AI systems.

Instead of repeatedly passing filters between dashboards, scripts, and external AI services, ContextWallet acts as a centralized context layer that can store business selections, maintain state across pages, create reusable snapshots, and prepare structured context payloads for AI assistants such as Databricks Genie.

While ContextWallet began as an experiment, it quickly evolved into something more interesting: a practical implementation of Context Engineering within SAP Analytics Cloud.

In the following sections, I will discuss the motivation behind this approach, the architecture of ContextWallet, and how it can help bridge the gap between traditional analytics and AI-powered decision-making.

The Missing Piece: Why Prompt Engineering Alone Is Not Enough

 

 

 

When discussing Generative AI, one concept appears almost everywhere: Prompt Engineering.

Prompt Engineering focuses on designing better instructions for AI systems in order to obtain more accurate, relevant, and useful responses. As Large Language Models became more capable, organizations quickly realized that the quality of the prompt significantly influences the quality of the response.

For example, instead of asking:

Explain revenue performance.

A more effective prompt might be:

Explain the revenue performance for Company Code 1000 in Fiscal Year 2025 and identify the top three contributing factors.

The second prompt provides more information and therefore allows the AI to generate a more focused response.

However, while experimenting with AI-driven analytics scenarios, I realized that prompt quality is only one part of the problem.

Even the most carefully designed prompt can fail if the AI lacks awareness of the user's current business context.

Consider a typical SAP Analytics Cloud dashboard used by a finance team.

A user may already have selected:

  • Company Code = 1000

  • Fiscal Year = 2025

  • Version = Actual

  • Currency = USD

  • Entity = EU_Corp

The dashboard understands these selections perfectly. Every chart, table, KPI, and calculation is already operating within this context.

Now the user asks:

Why is revenue decreasing?

From a human perspective, the question is completely valid because the context is already visible on the screen.

From the AI's perspective, however, the question is ambiguous.

Revenue for which company?

Which fiscal year?

Which version?

Which entity?

Without access to the dashboard context, the AI may either produce a generic answer or require the user to repeat information that already exists within the application.

This creates an inefficient user experience.

The user already provided the context through filters and selections, yet the AI remains unaware of it.

This is where I believe a new discipline becomes necessary.

Introducing Context Engineering

If Prompt Engineering is about optimizing the question, Context Engineering is about optimizing the information surrounding the question.

Context Engineering focuses on ensuring that AI systems receive the right business context before generating a response.

In analytics applications, context may include:

  • Active dashboard filters

  • User selections

  • Input control values

  • Reporting periods

  • Organizational hierarchies

  • Planning versions

  • Saved dashboard states

  • Business entities

Rather than repeatedly asking users to provide this information, the application should be capable of capturing and managing it automatically.

In other words, before asking:

Why is EBITDA decreasing?

the system should already know:

Company Code: 1000
Fiscal Year: 2025
Version: Actual
Currency: USD
Profit Center: PC001

This transforms AI interactions from generic conversations into context-aware business discussions.

The Role of Context in Enterprise AI

Unlike public AI use cases, enterprise AI systems rarely operate in isolation.

They exist within business applications, planning systems, reporting platforms, and analytical environments.

As a result, context often becomes more valuable than the prompt itself.

A simple question with rich business context can generate a highly relevant response.

Conversely, a sophisticated prompt with no context may generate a vague or inaccurate answer.

This realization became the foundation for ContextWallet.

Rather than treating context as something temporary and scattered across filters, pages, and widgets, I wanted to create a centralized context layer that could capture, preserve, and share business context across SAP Analytics Cloud applications and future AI integrations.

The objective was not simply to build another utility widget.

The objective was to experiment with Context Engineering as a practical architectural pattern for AI-powered analytics.

Introducing ContextWallet

ContextWallet

 

After identifying the need for a dedicated context layer within SAP Analytics Cloud, I began exploring possible implementation approaches.

The primary objective was not to create another utility widget or temporary storage mechanism. Instead, I wanted to build a reusable framework that could act as a centralized repository for business context and eventually serve as a bridge between SAC dashboards and AI-powered analytics systems.

This idea became ContextWallet.

ContextWallet is a custom SAP Analytics Cloud widget designed to capture, preserve, manage, and share business context across dashboard pages, applications, and external AI integrations.

At its core, ContextWallet acts as a lightweight context management engine that stores the current analytical state of an application in a structured format.

For example, a finance dashboard may contain the following business selections:

{
  "CompanyCode": "1000",
  "FiscalYear": "2025",
  "Version": "Actual",
  "Currency": "USD",
  "Entity": "EU_Corp"
}

Rather than keeping these values scattered across input controls, filters, dropdowns, and application variables, ContextWallet consolidates them into a single centralized context object.

This context can then be accessed by any component within the application and can be passed directly to AI systems whenever required.

Core Design Principles

While designing ContextWallet, I focused on four fundamental principles.

1. Centralized Context Management

Modern analytical applications often contain dozens of user selections spread across multiple pages.

Without a centralized mechanism, each component must independently manage and retrieve these values.

ContextWallet provides a single source of truth for business context.

Instead of querying multiple controls throughout the application, developers can retrieve the entire context through a single interface.

2. Cross-Page Persistence

One of the challenges in multi-page SAC applications is maintaining context while navigating between pages.

A user may define a specific analytical scenario on one page and expect it to remain available throughout the application.

ContextWallet addresses this by persisting context within the browser session, allowing information to remain accessible across multiple dashboard pages.

This creates a more seamless user experience while reducing the need for repetitive filter selections.

3. Snapshot-Based State Management

Analytical workflows frequently involve recurring review scenarios.

Examples include:

  • Month-End Reviews

  • Quarterly Business Reviews

  • Forecast Reviews

  • Budget Planning Sessions

To support these use cases, ContextWallet introduces a snapshot mechanism.

Snapshots allow users or developers to capture the complete dashboard context at a specific point in time and restore it later.

This capability enables reproducible analytical scenarios and creates the foundation for future context comparison capabilities.

4. AI-Ready Context Generation

Perhaps the most important design goal was preparing SAC applications for AI integrations.

Traditional dashboards and AI systems often operate independently, even though they are working with the same business data.

ContextWallet helps bridge this gap by transforming dashboard selections into structured context payloads that can be consumed by AI assistants.

For example, when a user asks:

Why is revenue decreasing?

ContextWallet can automatically attach the active business context before sending the request to an AI system.

Instead of receiving only a question, the AI receives both the question and the analytical context in which the question was asked.

This significantly improves the relevance and accuracy of generated responses.

5. Future AI Security and Session Management

As organizations move toward AI-powered analytics, context management will become increasingly important not only for usability but also for security and governance.

Today, ContextWallet primarily focuses on capturing and managing dashboard context. However, its architecture opens opportunities for future enhancements related to AI session management and role-based AI interactions.

In enterprise environments, different users often require access to different AI capabilities depending on their responsibilities.

For example:

  • Finance Analysts may interact with financial planning models.

  • Controllers may access variance analysis assistants.

  • FP&A teams may work with forecasting and budgeting copilots.

  • Executives may receive high-level strategic insights.

  • Operations teams may interact with supply chain or operational AI models.

Rather than exposing a single AI model to every user, future implementations could leverage ContextWallet as a session-aware context layer capable of managing AI routing and authorization.

A session identifier or secure context token could be maintained alongside business context, allowing applications to determine:

  • Which AI model should receive a request.

  • Which data domains are accessible.

  • Which analytical capabilities are available.

  • Which business entities a user is authorized to view.

In this scenario, ContextWallet would not simply store dashboard filters but would become part of a broader AI governance framework.

A simplified future architecture could look like this:

User
  │
  ▼
SAP Analytics Cloud
  │
  ▼
ContextWallet
  │
  ├── Business Context
  ├── Session Context
  ├── Security Context
  └── User Role Context
  │
  ▼
AI Routing Layer
  │
  ├── Finance AI Assistant
  ├── FP&A Copilot
  ├── Executive Insights Model
  └── Operations Intelligence Agent

Such an approach would allow organizations to provide tailored AI experiences while maintaining security, compliance, and governance requirements.

Although these capabilities are currently part of the long-term vision rather than the current implementation, they demonstrate how Context Engineering can evolve beyond dashboard state management and become a foundational layer for enterprise AI ecosystems.

More Than a Storage Widget

While ContextWallet currently provides capabilities such as context storage, retrieval, snapshot management, and AI payload generation, its long-term vision extends far beyond traditional state management.

At its core, ContextWallet represents an exploration of Context Engineering within SAP Analytics Cloud—a concept that I believe will become increasingly important as organizations integrate AI into their analytics ecosystems.

Today, business context is often fragmented across filters, input controls, application variables, and user selections. Human users can naturally understand this context because it is visible on the dashboard. AI systems, however, typically receive only the final question, without access to the business environment in which that question was asked.

ContextWallet attempts to bridge this gap by establishing a centralized context layer that can be shared across dashboards, applications, users, and AI services.

The objective is not simply to remember values.

The objective is to create a context-aware analytical environment where dashboards, business processes, and AI systems operate using a common understanding of the user's current business scenario.

Looking ahead, the same architecture could evolve beyond dashboard context management and become part of a broader AI governance and orchestration framework.

Future implementations could leverage context, session identifiers, role information, and security metadata to route requests to specialized AI models based on business function and user authorization.

For example:

  • Finance teams could interact with financial planning copilots.

  • FP&A users could access forecasting and variance analysis assistants.

  • Executives could receive strategic insight generation models.

  • Operations teams could utilize supply-chain-specific AI agents.

In such an architecture, ContextWallet would not only manage business context but also contribute to AI session management, security enforcement, model routing, and role-based AI experiences.

In other words, the future vision is not merely a smarter dashboard.

It is the creation of a shared context layer that enables analytics applications and AI systems to collaborate more effectively, securely, and intelligently.

The next section explores the technical architecture behind ContextWallet and demonstrates how it can act as an intermediary layer between SAP Analytics Cloud and AI platforms such as Databricks Genie.

Architecture and Technical Design

 

ContextWallet Architecture

 

 

After establishing the concept of Context Engineering, the next step was designing a practical implementation that could operate within SAP Analytics Cloud while remaining flexible enough to support future AI integrations.

The primary design objective was to create a lightweight, reusable context layer that could sit between dashboard interactions and AI systems.

Rather than allowing filters, selections, and application state to remain scattered throughout the application, ContextWallet centralizes this information into a structured context object that can be accessed by both SAC components and external services.

High-Level Architecture

At a high level, ContextWallet acts as an intermediary layer between user interactions and AI systems.

The workflow begins when users interact with dashboard components such as:

  • Input Controls

  • Dropdowns

  • Filters

  • Tables

  • Application Variables

  • Planning Selections

These interactions generate business context that is relevant to the current analytical session.

Instead of allowing each component to manage its own state independently, ContextWallet collects and stores this information in a centralized context repository.

The stored context can then be consumed by:

  • SAC Application Scripts

  • Dashboard Components

  • Snapshot Management Functions

  • AI Payload Builders

  • External AI Platforms such as Databricks Genie

This creates a single source of truth for business context throughout the application.

Context Lifecycle

The architecture follows a simple but effective lifecycle:

Step 1: Context Capture

User selections are captured from dashboard components and stored in ContextWallet.

For example:

ContextWallet_1.setContext({
    CompanyCode: "1000",
    FiscalYear: "2025",
    Version: "Actual",
    Currency: "USD"
});

At this stage, ContextWallet becomes aware of the user's analytical environment.

Step 2: Context Persistence

Once stored, the context remains available throughout the application lifecycle.

This enables:

  • Cross-page navigation

  • Multi-step workflows

  • Dashboard state retention

  • Consistent AI interactions

Instead of repeatedly requesting filter information from multiple controls, applications can retrieve the required context directly from ContextWallet.

Step 3: Context Enrichment

As users continue interacting with the dashboard, additional information can be added to the context.

For example:

ContextWallet_1.mergeContext({
    Entity: "EU_Corp",
    AccountType: "Revenue"
});

The context evolves alongside the user's analytical journey.

Step 4: Context Consumption

When an AI interaction occurs, ContextWallet combines the user's question with the active business context.

For example:

var payload =
ContextWallet_1.getAIPayload(
    "Why is revenue decreasing?"
);

Result:

{
  "question": "Why is revenue decreasing?",
  "context": {
    "CompanyCode": "1000",
    "FiscalYear": "2025",
    "Currency": "USD",
    "Entity": "EU_Corp",
    "AccountType": "Revenue"
  }
}

Rather than receiving an isolated question, the AI receives both the question and the business scenario in which it was asked.

Snapshot Engine

One of the core architectural components of ContextWallet is the Snapshot Engine.

Snapshots allow complete analytical states to be preserved and restored.

For example, a finance team conducting a month-end review may save the current context as:

ContextWallet_1.saveSnapshot(
    "MonthEndReview"
);

This snapshot captures the entire business context at that point in time.

Later, the same context can be restored using:

ContextWallet_1.loadSnapshot(
    "MonthEndReview"
);

This capability provides reproducibility for analytical workflows and establishes the foundation for future context comparison and AI memory scenarios.

Preparing for AI Integration

The architecture was intentionally designed with AI integrations in mind.

Traditional AI implementations typically receive only the user's prompt.

ContextWallet introduces an additional layer:

Question
+
Business Context
+
Session Context
+
Application State

This combination allows future AI systems to generate responses that are aware of:

  • Current dashboard filters

  • Active reporting periods

  • Business entities

  • User workflow state

  • Saved analytical scenarios

In essence, ContextWallet transforms dashboard interactions into structured knowledge that can be consumed by AI systems.

Extending the Architecture

Although the current implementation focuses on context management, the architecture has been designed to support future enhancements such as:

  • Context history tracking

  • Session management

  • AI routing

  • Role-based AI experiences

  • Context-aware security controls

  • Multi-agent orchestration

These capabilities could enable organizations to move beyond isolated AI assistants and toward fully context-aware analytics ecosystems.

The next section demonstrates how this architecture can be applied to a practical use case by integrating SAP Analytics Cloud with Databricks Genie through a shared context layer.

SAC + Databricks Genie: A Practical Context Engineering Use Case

The concept of Context Engineering becomes most valuable when we examine how SAP Analytics Cloud can interact with AI-powered analytics platforms such as Databricks Genie.

Databricks Genie enables users to ask business questions in natural language and receive insights directly from enterprise data. This significantly lowers the barrier to analytics by allowing business users to interact with data conversationally instead of relying solely on dashboards and reports.

 

 

 

However, when integrating SAC with Genie, a challenge immediately emerges.

The Context Gap

Consider a financial analyst working inside a SAC dashboard.

The analyst has already configured the dashboard using several business filters:

  • Company Code = 1000

  • Fiscal Year = 2025

  • Version = Actual

  • Currency = USD

  • Entity = EU_Corp

  • Account Type = Revenue

After reviewing the dashboard, the analyst asks:

Why is revenue lower than last year?

While the question appears complete from the user's perspective, it lacks the surrounding business context that exists within the dashboard.

Without access to the dashboard state, Genie may not know:

  • Which company is being analyzed

  • Which fiscal year is active

  • Which currency is being used

  • Which reporting version is selected

  • Which business entity is in focus

As a result, the AI system may generate a generic response or require additional clarification from the user.

This creates friction in the analytical workflow because users are forced to repeatedly provide information that already exists within the dashboard.

Context-Aware AI Interactions

This is where ContextWallet introduces a different approach.

Instead of sending only the user's question to Genie, ContextWallet can package both the question and the active dashboard context into a structured payload.

For example:

{
  "question": "Why is revenue lower than last year?",
  "context": {
    "CompanyCode": "1000",
    "FiscalYear": "2025",
    "Version": "Actual",
    "Currency": "USD",
    "Entity": "EU_Corp",
    "AccountType": "Revenue"
  }
}

From an AI perspective, this additional information is extremely valuable.

The question is no longer interpreted in isolation.

It is interpreted within the business scenario that the user is currently analyzing.

This allows AI systems to generate more focused, relevant, and actionable responses.

Improving User Experience

One of the most significant advantages of this approach is the reduction of repetitive user interactions.

Without Context Engineering:

User:
Why is revenue decreasing?

AI:
Which company?

User:
Company Code 1000.

AI:
Which fiscal year?

User:
2025.

AI:
Which reporting version?

User:
Actual.

With Context Engineering:

User:
Why is revenue decreasing?

AI:
Analyzes Company Code 1000,
Fiscal Year 2025,
Version Actual,
and provides a contextualized answer.

The user interacts naturally while the application provides the necessary context behind the scenes.

Beyond Questions and Answers

The potential value of ContextWallet extends beyond conversational analytics.

Future integrations could enable AI systems to access:

  • Current dashboard state

  • Saved snapshots

  • Historical analytical scenarios

  • User workflow context

  • Session metadata

  • Role information

This would allow AI systems to understand not only the current question but also the broader analytical journey of the user.

For example:

What changed since the Month-End Review?

Instead of treating this as a generic question, the AI could compare the current dashboard context with a previously saved snapshot and generate insights based on the differences.

Similarly, planning teams could compare forecast versions, executive teams could analyze strategic review snapshots, and finance teams could revisit previous reporting scenarios.

From Dashboards to Context-Aware Analytics

Traditional dashboards excel at presenting information.

AI systems excel at interpreting information.

However, without a shared context layer, these systems often operate independently.

ContextWallet attempts to bridge this gap by establishing a common context model that can be understood by both SAP Analytics Cloud and external AI platforms.

In this architecture, the dashboard becomes the source of business context, ContextWallet becomes the context management layer, and Databricks Genie becomes the intelligence layer.

Together, they create a foundation for a new generation of context-aware analytics applications where users can move seamlessly between dashboards, business workflows, and conversational AI experiences.

Future Vision: From Context Management to AI Orchestration

 

 

 

 

The current implementation of ContextWallet focuses on a relatively simple objective: capturing, preserving, and sharing business context within SAP Analytics Cloud.

However, while building and experimenting with the framework, it became apparent that the underlying concept could extend far beyond dashboard state management.

As organizations continue integrating AI into enterprise analytics, a new challenge emerges:

How do we manage, govern, and orchestrate multiple AI systems while maintaining security, context, and business relevance?

This is where the future vision of ContextWallet begins.

Beyond Dashboard Context

Today, ContextWallet primarily stores:

  • Dashboard filters

  • User selections

  • Application state

  • Analytical snapshots

  • AI payload context

In the future, the same architecture could be extended to manage additional layers of information such as:

  • Session Context

  • User Context

  • Security Context

  • Role Context

  • AI Routing Metadata

This would allow ContextWallet to evolve from a context repository into a centralized context orchestration layer.

AI Session Management

One of the most interesting future applications involves AI session management.

Enterprise AI interactions are rarely isolated events.

Users often engage in a sequence of related analytical conversations, building upon previous questions and insights.

For example:

Question 1:
Why is revenue decreasing?

Question 2:
Which products contributed most?

Question 3:
How does this compare with last quarter?

These questions are connected.

Without session awareness, each interaction must be treated independently.

With session-aware context management, AI systems can maintain continuity across analytical conversations and provide more relevant responses.

Future versions of ContextWallet could store session identifiers and maintain conversational context throughout the user's analytical journey.

Role-Based AI Experiences

Not every user requires access to the same AI capabilities.

A finance analyst, a controller, a planner, and an executive often have very different responsibilities and information requirements.

For example:

Finance Analyst

  • Variance Analysis

  • Financial Reporting

  • Cost Analysis

FP&A Team

  • Forecasting

  • Budget Planning

  • Scenario Simulation

Executive Leadership

  • Strategic Insights

  • KPI Summaries

  • Business Performance Reviews

Rather than exposing a single AI assistant to every user, future implementations could dynamically route requests to specialized AI systems based on role and authorization.

This would enable organizations to create tailored AI experiences while maintaining governance and security controls.

AI Routing and Model Selection

As AI ecosystems mature, organizations are unlikely to rely on a single model.

Instead, different AI systems may be optimized for different tasks.

For example:

Financial Analysis Model
        │
Forecasting Model
        │
Planning Copilot
        │
Executive Insights Assistant
        │
Operational Intelligence Agent

Future versions of ContextWallet could act as an intelligent routing layer.

Based on context, user role, and session metadata, requests could automatically be directed to the most appropriate AI system.

This would allow users to interact with a unified interface while the platform manages the complexity behind the scenes.

Security and Governance

Enterprise AI adoption introduces important governance requirements.

Organizations must ensure that users only access information relevant to their responsibilities.

ContextWallet could potentially support this through:

  • Role-aware context management

  • Secure session identifiers

  • Access-control metadata

  • AI authorization rules

For example, an executive assistant might receive organization-wide insights, while a finance analyst receives only information related to their assigned entities.

This approach would help ensure that AI interactions remain aligned with existing security and compliance frameworks.

Towards a Context-Aware Analytics Ecosystem

The long-term vision is not simply a smarter dashboard.

The vision is a context-aware analytics ecosystem where dashboards, applications, business processes, and AI systems operate using a shared understanding of the business environment.

In such an ecosystem:

  • Dashboards generate context.

  • ContextWallet manages context.

  • AI systems consume context.

  • Business users receive contextualized insights.

Instead of repeatedly explaining the business scenario to every application and AI assistant, context becomes a reusable enterprise asset.

Getting Started with ContextWallet

For readers interested in experimenting with ContextWallet, the project is available as an open-source SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget.

GitHub Repository

Source Code:

https://github.com/sampurno417/ContextWallet

The repository contains:

  • ContextWallet Widget Source Code

  • Widget Manifest (JSON)

  • Production Bundle

  • Documentation

  • Example Usage Patterns

Installation

Step 1: Download the Widget Manifest

Open the repository and download the latest ContextWallet JSON manifest.

Step 2: Import the Widget into SAP Analytics Cloud

Navigate to:

Analytics Designer
→ Custom Widgets
→ Add Custom Widget
→ Upload JSON Manifest

Once imported, ContextWallet becomes available within your SAC application.

Step 3: Add ContextWallet to the Application

Drag and drop the widget onto any page.

The widget itself does not need to be visible to end users and can be hidden after being added to the application.

ContextWallet is primarily designed as a backend utility component accessed through Analytics Designer scripting.

Basic Usage

Store Context

ContextWallet_1.setContext({
    CompanyCode: "1000",
    FiscalYear: "2025",
    Currency: "USD"
});

Retrieve Context

var context =
ContextWallet_1.getContext();

console.log(context);

Add Additional Fields

ContextWallet_1.addContextField(
    "Entity",
    "EU_Corp"
);

Merge Context

ContextWallet_1.mergeContext({
    AccountType: "Revenue"
});

Working with Snapshots

Save the current dashboard state:

ContextWallet_1.saveSnapshot(
    "MonthEndReview"
);

Retrieve available snapshots:

var snapshots =
ContextWallet_1.getSnapshots();

Restore a snapshot:

ContextWallet_1.loadSnapshot(
    "MonthEndReview"
);

Preparing AI Payloads

ContextWallet can generate structured payloads suitable for AI integrations.

var payload =
ContextWallet_1.getAIPayload(
    "Why is revenue decreasing?"
);

console.log(payload);

Example output:

{
  "question": "Why is revenue decreasing?",
  "context": {
    "CompanyCode": "1000",
    "FiscalYear": "2025",
    "Currency": "USD",
    "Entity": "EU_Corp"
  }
}

This payload can be passed to external AI systems such as Databricks Genie, custom LLM applications, or enterprise copilots.

Current Status

ContextWallet is currently an experimental framework focused on exploring Context Engineering concepts within SAP Analytics Cloud.

The project continues to evolve, with future enhancements planned around:

  • AI Session Management

  • Role-Based AI Routing

  • Security Context

  • Context History

  • Multi-Agent AI Integrations

  • Databricks Genie Connectivity

Feedback, suggestions, and collaboration ideas are always welcome.

Conclusion

As Generative AI becomes increasingly embedded within enterprise analytics platforms, context will play a critical role in determining the quality, relevance, and trustworthiness of AI-generated insights.

ContextWallet began as an experiment in solving a practical problem within SAP Analytics Cloud: preserving and sharing dashboard context.

Along the way, it revealed a broader architectural concept that I refer to as Context Engineering.

While the current implementation focuses on context management, snapshots, and AI payload generation, the underlying idea has the potential to evolve into a foundational layer for future AI-enabled analytics applications.

The journey is still at an early stage, but one thing is becoming increasingly clear:

In the era of enterprise AI, understanding the question is important.

Understanding the context behind the question is essential.

This project was developed as part of my exploration into SAP Analytics Cloud, AI-assisted analytics, and Databricks Genie integrations. I hope it encourages further discussion around Context Engineering and the role of context-aware architectures in the future of enterprise analytics.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud: від таблиць Excel до розумних дашбордів
entry.content:
Скільки годин на тиждень ваші колеги витрачають на зведення даних із різних систем в Excel, щоб потім зробити звіт для керівництва? Якщо відповідь «занадто багато» - самий час познайомитися з SAP Analytics Cloud.

Що таке SAP Analytics Cloud?

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) - це хмарна платформа для бізнес-аналітики, планування та прогнозування від SAP. Вона об'єднує три функції, які раніше потребували окремих інструментів: BI-звітність, корпоративне планування та предиктивна аналітика - все в одному браузерному застосунку.

Три стовпи SAP Analytics Cloud

• Business Intelligence - інтерактивні дашборди, аналіз у режимі реального часу, Stories (розповіді на основі даних)

• Planning - бюджетування, прогнозування, сценарне планування. Замінює громіздкі Excel-моделі

• Predictive Analytics - вбудовані ML-моделі для прогнозів без участі data scientist

Підключення до SAP-систем

SAC природно інтегрується з усіма SAP-джерелами: S/4HANA, BW/4HANA, Datasphere, SuccessFactors. Дані оновлюються в реальному часі або за розкладом. Це означає, що фінансовий директор бачить актуальний баланс не раз на місяць після закриття, а щомиті.

💡 Порівняння: Power BI і Tableau - потужні інструменти для загальної аналітики. SAC виграє там, де потрібна глибока інтеграція з SAP-даними, вбудоване планування та знання бізнес-семантики SAP. Якщо у вас SAP - SAC є природним вибором.

SAC для нетехнічних користувачів

Одна з ключових переваг SAC - доступність для бізнес-аналітиків без технічних знань. Drag-and-drop побудова дашбордів, автоматичні підказки системи («Чому зріс цей показник?»), Smart Insights та Smart Predict дозволяють отримувати аналітику без допомоги IT-відділу.

З чого почати?

1. SAP пропонує безкоштовний Trial-акаунт SAC на 90 днів - спробуйте підключити тестові дані

2. Почніть з побудови одного дашборду для реального бізнес-завдання вашої компанії

3. Вивчіть SAP Learning Journey «SAP Analytics Cloud» на офіційній платформі

Компанії, що переходять від Excel-звітності до SAC, зазвичай вражені не лише зручністю, а й тим, скільки помилок і розбіжностей ховалося в їхніх старих таблицях. Дані з однієї системи - надійніші за дані з десяти файлів.

entry.title: Horizon Morning Becomes the New SAP Analytics Cloud Default Theme in Q4 2026
entry.content:
With the Q4 2026 release of SAP Analytics Cloud in November, we are excited to announce an important update in our user experience strategy: Horizon Morning will become the new default theme. For cusutomers on fast track tenants, the update will be available in early August already.

This change is part of our continued investment in aligning the interface of SAP Analytics Cloud with other SAP solutions, bringing a modernized visual language, improved accessibility, and enhanced usability to analytics users across the platform.

In addition to Horizon Morning, users also have access to a dark theme as well as high contrast theme and an automatic option that choses the theme closest to your OS setting. Find how to change the theme in the Horizon release blog.

Automatic Rollout for Users Without Custom Themes

With the Q4 2026 upgrade, all users who have not customized their theme either at the tenant level or user level will be automatically switched to the new Horizon Morning theme.

Users and tenants with custom theme selections will retain their current configuration. No changes will be made to override your customization.

This ensures a smooth and predictable transition while respecting existing preferences and branding setups.

Belize Remains Available (For Now)

Although Horizon Morning becomes the new default, the Belize theme will still be available in the theme selector. Users may switch back to Belize at any time if they prefer the familiar experience.

However, as part of our long‑term UI evolution, Belize is planned for deprecation in the coming quarters. Because of this, we encourage all users and administrators to begin transitioning to Horizon.

Why Move to Horizon Morning?

The Horizon design system brings several advantages:

  • A clean, contemporary visual aesthetic optimized for clarity
  • Improved contrast, spacing, and component behavior
  • Increased accessibility compliance
  • A consistent look and feel across the SAP product ecosystem

These enhancements ensure that users can work more efficiently and comfortably across scenarios.

entry.title: Building a Customer Master Data Input Form Using SAC Scripting
entry.content:
Business Scenario / Problem Statement

Long-range forecasting often needs to plan for things that haven't happened yet — for example, revenue for customers a company intends to onboard in the future. Since these customers don't exist in the planning master data yet, users need a way to create them directly, along with control over attributes like their planning status.

This gave rise to two problems I needed to solve:

  1. Creating new master data on the fly: business users needed a controlled way to add a new customer to the planning model dimension and maintain attributes like Customer Status (Active/Inactive for planning) whenever required — without needing broader access to the planning model itself.
  2. Auto-incrementing the Customer ID: since customer IDs follow a simple numeric sequence, manually asking users to key in the next available ID is error-prone and invites duplicates. The form needed to work out the next ID on its own each time it loads.

With the help of SACs inbuilt scripting capabilities — it gives a limited set of business users a simple, governed way to create a new planning-only customer record with an auto-generated ID, and to update attributes on that record later.

Solution Overview

I built a two-part Story using SAC's Advanced Mode scripting capabilities:

  • Create Customer Master Data — auto-generates the next available Customer ID, captures description Customer Name, and attributes UAR Number, Customer Status, and UAR Status, and writes a new member into the Customer dimension.
  • Update Customer Master Data — lets a user pick an existing (including previously self-created) customer from a dropdown and update just the Customer Status attribute.

Both forms use the SAC Planning Model scripting APIs getMembers, getMember, createMembers, and updateMembers against the Customer dimension of the planning model, all scripted directly on the widgets within the Story canvas.

Script Variables

A handful of script variables drive the whole application:

VariableTypePurpose
allmemberPlanningModelMemberHolds the JSON payload passed to createMembers / updateMembers
customerIdFilterValueReference to the customer being worked on
NewCustomerNumberNumberNumeric form of the next available Customer ID, used for the increment logic
NewCustomerStringStringString form of NewCustomerNumber, since dimension member IDs are strings
 

Walkthrough:

OnInitialization of page event:

When the page loads, the same block of logic serves both forms:

Application.showBusyIndicator("Initializing new customer...");
var customerDim = PlanningModel_1.getMembers("Customer");

For the Update form, the dropdown is rebuilt from scratch and seeded with a placeholder instruction:

Dropdown_1.removeAllItems();
Dropdown_1.addItem("Select a Customer", "Select a Customer");
Dropdown_1.setSelectedKey("Select a Customer");

for (var i = 1; i < customerDim.length; i++) {
    Dropdown_1.addItem(customerDim[i].id, customerDim[i].description);
}

Starting the loop at index 1 skips the technical "not assigned" default member that most dimensions carry at index 0 — a small but important detail if you don't want that showing up as a selectable customer.

For the Create form, the trick is generating the next Customer ID automatically. Since new customers are always appended with an incrementing numeric ID, the last entry in the dimension is always the highest existing ID:

var LatestEntry = customerDim[customerDim.length - 1].id;
NewCustomerNumber = ConvertUtils.stringToNumber(LatestEntry) + 1;
NewCustomerString = ConvertUtils.numberToString(NewCustomerNumber);
Text_9.applyText(NewCustomerString);

The ID needs to round-trip between number and string because incrementing a string doesn't work the way you'd want ("740" + 1 isn't "741" in JavaScript), but the dimension member ID itself has to be written back as a string.

OnClick event of Button (Submit) - Creating a New Customer:

On submit of the create form, the values entered in the input fields and radio button groups are packaged into a single member object and pushed to the model:

var custName = InputField_2.getValue().trim();
var UARNo = InputField_3.getValue().trim();
var CustomerStatus = RadioButtonGroup_1.getSelectedKey();
var UARStatus = RadioButtonGroup_2.getSelectedKey();

allmember = ({
  id: NewCustomerString,
  description: custName,
  properties: { UAR_NO: UARNo, CustomerStatus: CustomerStatus, UAR_Status: UARStatus }
});

PlanningModel_1.createMembers("Customer", allmember);

Once the new member is created, the form resets itself and — this is the part I like — increments the counter again and re-populates the next available Customer ID, so the user can keep creating customers back-to-back without ever touching the ID field manually:

Dropdown_1.addItem(NewCustomerString, custName);
NewCustomerNumber = NewCustomerNumber + 1;
NewCustomerString = ConvertUtils.numberToString(NewCustomerNumber);
Text_9.applyText(NewCustomerString);

Note the dropdown on the Update form is also updated in the same step, so a customer created in this session is immediately available for editing without a page refresh.

OnSelect event of Dropdown- Selecting the customer to updates its properties:

When a user picks a customer from the dropdown on the Update form, the script reads that member's current properties and reflects them on screen — in this case, setting the Active/Inactive radio button to match the stored CustomerStatus:

var selectedId = Dropdown_1.getSelectedKey();

if (selectedId === "Select a Customer") {
    RadioButtonGroup_3.setSelectedKey("Active");
    return;
}

var customerDim = PlanningModel_1.getMember("Customer", selectedId);
RadioButtonGroup_3.setSelectedKey(customerDim.properties.CustomerStatus);

OnClick event of Button(Submit) - Updating properties of Customer:

On submit, only the changed attribute is sent back — there's no need to resend the description or other unrelated properties:

var custId = Dropdown_1.getSelectedKey();
var CustomerStatus = RadioButtonGroup_3.getSelectedKey();

if (custId === "Select a Customer") {
  Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error, "Customer ID is required.");
  return;
}

allmember = ({
  id: custId,
  properties: { CustomerStatus: CustomerStatus }
});

PlanningModel_1.updateMembers("Customer", allmember);

This is a nice illustration of updateMembers behavior: you don't have to pass the full member object, only the ID plus the properties you actually want to change.

Conclusion

Planning cycles don't wait for master data to catch up, and this is a simple example of how a bit of scripting inside a Story can close that gap without any dependency on the source system. With just two forms and a handful of script variables, business users get a self-service way to onboard a new customer for planning purposes — with the ID handled automatically — and to keep that customer's status current as plans evolve.

entry.title: DSP Models used in SAC Stories (BW-like where-used)
entry.content:
Having difficulties to identify impact of failed DSP Loads to SAC Stories? 
Having difficulties to identify how many SAC Stories need to be adjusted with an DSP Model Dimension change for example? 
Looking for where-used like BW feature? Impact analyzes? Lineage from DSP to SAC. 

Manual Steps:
1. Get all Stories and all Models data into a JSON: https://<your-tenant>/api/v1/stories?include=models
2. Save the results into a file
3. Process the file with a parser

I used python and have had the script vibe-code generated: I asked: "create parser python script on an example (first Story from the JSON) with delimiter: "{"name":" to "{"name":" and output format for (for example) DevOps Wiki. Provide a List of Models for each Story (use models: and description:)_. Can be enhanced with: create link to SAC Story providing the base-link as well.

Can be automated, but... bearer token is needed to fetch the result using a notebook, can be downloaded from browser, but SAC has only 8 hours validity. Since the Story to Model is static, manual way should be enough.

Simple and efficient. API: /api/v1/stories?include=models helps not only to create dynamic jump links to another SAC Stories, but also can be used for semi-automated documentation

Example: 

{"name":"<STORY NAME>","description":"<STORY DESC>","createdBy":"<USER_ID>","id":"<STORY_ID>","created":"<TIMESTAMP>","changed":"<TIMESTAMP>","isTemplate":false,"isSample":false,"models":[{"description":"<MODEL1_ID>","id":"<MODEL1_INTERNAL_ID>","remoteConnection":{"connectionType":"DIRECT","systemType":"DWC","name":"<CONNECTION_ID>","protocol":"HTTPS","host":"<URL>","port":"443","id":"<CONNECTION_ID>","scpAccount":null},"isPlanning":false},{"description":"<MODEL2_ID>","id":"<MODEL2_INTERNAL_ID>","remoteConnection":{"connectionType":"DIRECT","systemType":"DWC","name":"<CONNECTION_ID>","protocol":"HTTPS","host":"<URL>","port":"443","id":"<CONNECTION_ID>","scpAccount":null},"isPlanning":false}],"changedBy":"<USER_ID>","openURL":"<URL>"},{"name":.... 

 

Main blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/bw-vs-datasphere-dsp-amp-sac/ba-p/1428...

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud Seamless Planning入門 - Part2 : Seamless Planningで計画モデルを作成してみよう
entry.content:
このブログシリーズは、SAP Business Data CloudのSeamless Planningの全体像を一通り把握していただくことを目的としています。

このシリーズは、以下で構成されます。
- Part1 : Seamless Planningとは
- Part2 : Seamless Planningで計画モデルを作成してみよう（本ブログ）
- Part3 : Seamless PlanningでSAP Datasphereの実績ビューをライブバージョンで参照しよう
- to be continued ...

本ブログ「Part2 : Seamless Planningで計画モデルを作成してみよう」では、実際にSeamless Planningモデルを作成する手順と、作成後にSAP Datasphere側でデータを確認する方法を解説します。
 

目次

1. Seamless Planningで計画モデルを作成
2. ファクトテーブルをSAP Datasphereに公開
3. SAP Datasphereでデータを確認

1. Seamless Planningで計画モデルを作成

SAP Analytics Cloudの計画モデルはデータ保存先を「SAP Analytics Cloud」もしくは「SAP Datasphere」に選択することができます。SAP Datasphereを選択することでSeamless Planningの機能をご利用いただくことができます。

手順：

1.1. SAP Analytics Cloudのナビゲーションバーから 「モデラ」＞新規作成「モデル」＞ 「次へ」を選択します。（今回は「空のモデルから開始」の場合をご案内します）

 1.2. データ保管場所として「SAP Datasphere」を選択し、使用したいスペースを選択して「作成」をクリックします。

1.3.  以降はメジャーやディメンションの設定を行い、モデルを保存します。

これでまずはSAP Datasphereにデータが保管されるSAP Analytics Cloudの計画モデルが作成できました。

 

2. ファクトテーブルをSAP Datasphereに公開

 

SAP Analytics Cloud上で計画モデルを作成・保存しただけでは、SAP DatasphereのData Builder上からファクトテーブルを参照することができません。SAP Analytics Cloud側から「公開」操作を行う必要があります。この操作によって、SAP DatasphereのモデラがSAP Analytics Cloudの計画データをビューや分析モデルのソースとして利用できるようになります。

手順：

2.1. SAP Analytics Cloudで1で作成した計画モデルを開き、右側のモデル詳細パネルにて「SAP Datasphereでの公開」＞「編集...」をクリックします。

 
2.2. 「SAP Datasphereでデータファンデーショnをファクトテーブルとして...1」にチェックが入ってることを確認し、ファクトテーブルの名前を入力して「OK」をクリックします 。

2.3. モデルを保存します。

 これでSAP Analytics Cloudの計画データをSAP Datasphereでも使用できるようになりました。

3. SAP Datasphereでデータを確認

SAP Datasphere側にファクトテーブルが格納されているかを確認してみましょう。

手順：

3.1. SAP Analytics Cloudの右上「製品切り替え」よりSAP Datasphereに切り替えます。

 3.2. ナビゲーションバーの「データビルダ」より対象のスペースを選択すると、2で公開したファクトテーブルを確認することができました。

SAP Datasphereにて、SAP Analytics Cloudの計画データをビューや分析モデルのソースとして利用できるようになります。 

 

以上、Seamless Planningモデルの作成とSAP Datasphereでのデータ確認方法を解説しました。次回のブログでは、SAP Datasphereのビューをライブバージョンとして計画モデルに接続し、実績データをリアルタイム参照する方法を解説します。

参考資料・ブログ

- Expose Objects for Consumption in SAP Datasphere | SAP Help Portal
- Seamless Planning with Datasphere & SAC: Seamless Planning in the Cloud

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud: A Look at 18 Months of Customer-Driven Deliveries
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud's (SAC) Influence Campaign consistently attracts strong engagement from customers and SAP colleagues alike — a testament to the product's active user base and the high expectations that come with it.

In short, the SAC engineering team closed 167 influence requests representing a combined 4,089 customer votes across six release quarters through 2026 QRC2. This blog walks through what we built, why it matters, and where to learn more.

It's also worth noting that we delivered a significant number of additional features gathered through other channels, which are not covered here.

 

New Table Builder Experience

The brand-new Table Builder Panel brings the table design experience in line with that of Charts and Geo Visualizations, streamlining the story creation workflow. Beyond the builder itself, we delivered a range of enhancements covering sorting, ranking, data blending, parent and total positioning, and input readiness for planning scenarios.

 

 

New Table Builder Experience
